commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces `fetchCopilotUsage` success-body parsing with `readProviderJsonResponse` and adds an oversized-stream cancellation regression test.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +41. Total +45 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main calls `await res.json()` on successful Copilot usage responses, and the proposed oversized `ReadableStream` path exercises that unbounded read; I did not execute it because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated code repair is indicated; the remaining blocker is contributor real-behavior proof and ordinary maintainer review.

target.title: fix(github-copilot): bound usage response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`fetchCopilotUsage` in `extensions/github-copilot/usage.ts` reads the usage response with an unbounded `await res.json()`. The shared fetch layer applies an abort timeout but does **not** bound the body, so a misbehaving or compromised endpoint can stream an arbitrarily large response that is fully buffered into memory — a memory-pressure / hang (OOM) vector on the usage path.

## Evidence

Reuses the shared bounded reader `readProviderJsonResponse` (16 MiB cap, cancels the stream on overflow) — same pattern as the recently merged #95218 / #96027 / #96038. Plugin-scoped, no new abstraction; brings GitHub Copilot usage in line with the other providers' bounded JSON reads.

Real behavior proof (live vitest, not mocked): a real `ReadableStream` streaming a body past the cap is fed through `fetchCopilotUsage`, asserting it rejects with `github-copilot-usage: JSON response exceeds ...`, the stream is canceled, and `bytesPulled < cap` (not fully buffered).

```
Tests  29 passed (29)
```
